page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core metrics placing in the top 25%. The processor's cryptographic capabilities are highlighted by strong Passmark CPU encryption scores and hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is highly efficient at 4066.4 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Additionally, the instance demonstrates strong performance in database operations, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which reached 61.66 million operations per second.

From a resource-density perspective, the n2d-highcpu-64 offers a cost-efficient profile for CPU-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. The 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio avoids the expense of unutilized memory. However, the lack of local NVMe storage means all storage operations must traverse the network, which may introduce latency. The absence of AVX-512 instruction support also limits its efficiency in certain vector-heavy mathematical computations compared to alternative architectures.

This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as high-throughput web serving, batch processing, CPU-based image processing, and Redis-based caching layers. It is also appropriate for moderate database operations and software compilation tasks where parallel CPU execution is highly utilized but memory requirements remain under 64 GB.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 64 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, utilizing dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processors. It features 32 physical cores with multithreading, a 1.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio, and lacks local storage or integrated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, high-speed cached memory bandwidth, and excellent Redis SET performance at 61.66 million operations per second. However, uncached memory bandwidth and text processing speeds remain average. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory allocations. It is highly suited for parallel batch processing, web serving, cryptographic operations, and distributed caching.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, designed for high-throughput parallel workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and dedicated AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC compute instance optimized for parallel processing, cryptography, and high-throughput caching workloads with a lean memory footprint.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 64 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
High-throughput Redis caching reaching 61.66 million operations per second
Lean 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio optimized for compute-heavy tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for batch processing, web serving, and parallel compilation
